q.en: At the start of Chapter 19, what does the regiment do?
q.zh: 第19章开头，这个团做了什么？

options.en: They go to sleep in the grass
The regiment begins a charge
options.zh: 这个团发起了一轮冲锋

options.en: They settle down quietly to eat their supper
options.zh: 他们安静坐下沉沉吃晚饭

options.en: They sing a happy song together
options.zh: 他们一起唱了首欢快的歌

explain.en: The line fell slowly forward and the regiment began its journey—a charge.
explain.zh: 队伍缓缓前倾，这个团开始了它的行程——一次冲锋。

q.en: During the charge, what happens to the color sergeant?
q.zh: 冲锋途中，掌旗军士长遭遇了什么？

options.en: He cooks a meal for all
He writes a letter home
He leads a cheer for the men according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他带头为弟兄们欢呼（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He is hit and falls down dead

explain.en: The color sergeant flinched as if struck by a bludgeon and became motionless—dead.
explain.zh: 掌旗军士长像被钝器击中般一颤，随即不动——死了。

q.en: The sergeant is struck by a 'bludgeon'. What is a bludgeon?
q.zh: 军士长被“bludgeon”击中。bludgeon 是什么？

options.en: A heavy blunt club
options.zh: 一根沉重、没有刃的棍棒

options.en: A small cup that is used for coffee
A kind of fast horse
A soft, warm blanket

explain.en: Bludgeon is a blunt weapon such as a club with a weighted end.
explain.zh: bludgeon 是“一头加重的钝器，如棍棒”。

q.en: After the sergeant falls, what do Henry and Wilson do?
q.zh: 军士长倒下后，亨利和威尔逊做了什么？

options.en: They run off to the rear
They hide behind a tree
They grab the flag from the dead man
options.zh: 他们从死者的手里夺过了旗

options.en: They sit and rest a while according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们坐下歇了一会儿（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The two youths wrench the flag furiously from the dead sergeant's grasp.
explain.zh: 两个年轻人从死去的军士长手里，猛力夺过了旗。

q.en: In Chapter 20, what has happened to much of the regiment?
q.zh: 第20章里，这个团的大部分发生了什么？

options.en: It has cooked a feast
It has gone to sleep
It has grown twice as big according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它壮大了一倍（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: It has crumbled away and shrunk

explain.en: The youth and friend saw that much of the regiment had crumbled away.
explain.zh: 年轻人和朋友看到，这个团的大部分已经溃散了。

q.en: How does the charge show unity—or the loss of it?
q.zh: 这次冲锋如何表现了“团结”，又如何表现了团结的丧失？

options.en: The men move together yet later break
options.zh: 弟兄们曾一同推进，可后来还是散了

options.en: They quietly refuse to speak a single word to each other
options.zh: 他们静静地、彼此一句话都不肯说

options.en: They all run off alone
They eat the whole day

explain.en: They charged as one mass, but the command later split and the regiment crumbled.
explain.zh: 他们曾作为一个整体冲锋，可指挥后来被打散，团队也就溃了。

q.en: Henry wears a scowl of 'mortification'. What is mortification?
q.zh: 亨利挂着一脸“mortification”的怒容。mortification 是什么？

options.en: A loud, angry shout
Deep shame or embarassment
A soft, calm sleep
A bright and very happy smiling face
options.zh: 一张明亮、非常快乐的笑脸

explain.zh: mortification 意为“极度的难堪、羞耻”。

q.en: When they finally face the enemy again, what happens?
q.zh: 当他们终于再次面对敌人时，发生了什么？

options.en: The foe retreats; men cheer
The men cook a meal
The enemy soundly wins the whole field
options.zh: 敌人干脆赢下了整片战场

options.en: The men all go to sleep

explain.en: The blows of the foe grew weak; the blue men saw an empty stage and rejoiced.
explain.zh: 敌方的打击渐弱；蓝衣士兵看到空场，欢喜起来。

q.en: Why does the author show the flag passed from the dead sergeant?
q.zh: 作者为什么要写“旗从死去的军士长手里传下来”？

options.en: To list all the officers' names
options.zh: 为了列出所有军官的名字

options.en: To show duty carries on past one man
options.zh: 为了表现职责会越过一个人、继续传下去

options.en: To give a step-by-step lesson in how to cook a simple camp meal
options.zh: 为了一步步教人如何在营地煮顿简餐

options.en: To teach a lesson about riding horses

explain.en: Henry and Wilson seize the flag, showing the group's purpose lives on through them.
explain.zh: 亨利和威尔逊接过旗，显示了集体的目标经由他们延续下去。

q.en: How is the lieutenant hurt in this part?
q.zh: 在这一部分里，中尉是怎么受伤的？

options.en: He twists his ankle
He is handed a shiny new medal of honor
options.zh: 他获颁一枚鲜亮的新荣誉勋章

options.en: He is shot in the arm
He catches a cold

explain.en: The lieutenant, last in the mass, had been shot in the arm; it hung rigid.
explain.zh: 中尉落在队伍最后，胳膊中了一枪，僵硬地垂着。

prompt.en: The flag is passed from a fallen soldier to Henry and Wilson. What does it mean to carry on a job when someone else can't? Can you think of a time a task was handed to you?
prompt.zh: 旗从倒下的士兵手里交到了亨利和威尔逊手上。当别人无法继续时，接手一项任务意味着什么？你能想起某次任务交到你手上的时刻吗？

en: Recall the sergeant fell but the flag did not touch the ground.
Henry and Wilson stepped in so the group kept its symbol.
Think of a turn where you took over something important for others.
zh: 回想军士长倒下，但旗没有落地。
亨利和威尔逊顶上，保住了队伍的旗帜。
想想你曾为别人接手某项重要事的时刻。
